_meta.version_notes: v2.0 adds EP-19 (Compliance), EP-20 (Security), EP-21 (Vehicle Type), EP-22 (GA4 Analytics), EP-23 (Multi-Language), EP-24 (Launch Readiness). 20 new stories (US-081 to US-100). EP-18 repurposed as Internal BI (no third-party API).

epics.name: Authentication & User Management
epics.description: Consumer registration (phone OTP, social login), business owner login, profile setup, session management.
epics.notes: Blocks all other epics. Must ship first.

epics.name: Discovery & Search
epics.description: Consumer browses and searches businesses by category, district, date, party size. Filters, ranking algorithm, business profile display.
epics.notes: Ranking algo needs real data to tune. Launch with proximity + rating.

epics.name: Booking Engine
epics.description: Core slot availability, tailored category journeys (restaurant, salon), slot hold (Redis), booking state machine, conflict detection, reschedule, cancel.
epics.notes: Most complex epic. Redis slot locking is critical path.

epics.name: Payment & Escrow
epics.description: Paymob integration (card, InstaPay, Fawry, Vodafone Cash, Meeza), deposit capture, escrow, payout, refunds, receipts.
epics.notes: WABA registration runs in parallel. Paymob sandbox from Day 1.

epics.name: No-Show & Cancellation
epics.description: No-show detection job (every 15 min), penalty split (75/25), card-on-file, dispute window, ops resolution flow.
epics.notes: Detection job is cron on ECS. Dispute flow needs admin console.

epics.name: Notifications
epics.description: WhatsApp Business API (360dialog), SMS fallback (Twilio), push (FCM), booking confirmation, reminders (24h+2h), review prompt, no-show alerts.
epics.notes: WABA template approval: submit at Week 1. 2–4 week approval time.

epics.name: Business Dashboard
epics.description: Business owner web PWA: calendar view, booking management, availability setup, deposit/policy config, staff management (salon), analytics, manual bookings.
epics.notes: Tablet-first PWA. RTL sidebar on right.

epics.name: Consumer Mobile App
epics.description: React Native iOS + Android app: home feed, search, booking journeys, my bookings, profile, settings, RTL Arabic-first UI.
epics.notes: App Store + Play Store submission: allow 2 weeks before launch.

epics.name: Admin Console
epics.description: Internal ops tool: business verification, dispute resolution, platform health dashboard, featured listing management, refund execution, user management.
epics.notes: Internal web app. No App Store. Ops team uses from Day 1.

epics.name: Reviews & Loyalty (Phase 1 Foundation)
epics.description: Post-visit review prompt (WhatsApp), verified-booker-only reviews, moderation queue, rating display on profile. Loyalty points as placeholder schema only.
epics.notes: Full loyalty program (cross-category points) is Phase 2.

epics.name: Sports Court Booking
epics.description: Court category journey: sport type, duration, slot, player count, equipment rental, squad share card. Business config: court inventory, hourly pricing.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Court booking engine extends core EP-03 with duration slots.

epics.name: Gaming Cafe Booking
epics.description: Gaming cafe category: station type (PC/console/VR), duration, group room, genre preference. Business config: station inventory, block durations.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Similar slot logic to courts.

epics.name: Car Wash Booking
epics.description: Car wash category: vehicle type, service package, drop-off vs wait, bay assignment, estimated completion time, ready notification.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Adds vehicle type data model — see EP-21.

epics.name: Dynamic Pricing Engine
epics.description: Business owner configures surge multipliers for peak hours, demand-based auto-pricing, last-minute discount rules. Platform enforces and executes.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Requires booking volume data to tune algorithms.

epics.name: WhatsApp Bot Onboarding
epics.description: WhatsApp Business bot for zero-friction business onboarding. Business can start accepting bookings via WhatsApp in 10 minutes without web dashboard.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Highest-impact supply acquisition lever.

epics.name: Multi-Category Loyalty Program
epics.description: Consumer earns points across all categories. Points redeemable across categories. Loyalty tiers. Business loyalty analytics.
epics.notes: Phase 3. Cross-category points are the core retention moat.

epics.name: Featured Listings & Marketplace
epics.description: Business owner purchases featured placement slots. Consumer-facing discovery tab. Featured badge on profile. Marketplace ranking controls in admin.
epics.notes: Phase 2. Revenue unlock for platform.

epics.name: Internal BI & Ops Intelligence
epics.description: Internal analytics layer (Metabase/Redash on RDS read replica): booking density by district/hour, category growth curves, payment method mix, no-show trends. Internal ops only — no third-party API.
epics.notes: Repurposed from Data Intelligence API. Internal only. No external access. Viable from 10K bookings.

epics.name: Compliance & Privacy
epics.description: Egypt PDPL 2020 compliance: privacy policy acceptance at signup, consumer data export (JSON/PDF), account deletion with PII anonymisation, data retention schedule (24-month purge), ops audit log for all admin actions.
epics.notes: Legal requirement before App Store submission. Privacy policy URL mandatory for iOS review. PDPL 2020 in force.

epics.name: Security Hardening
epics.description: OTP velocity throttling (max 3 requests/10 min per phone), Paymob webhook idempotency enforcement, immutable admin console audit log (actor, timestamp, IP, reason), pen-test scope definition and formal sign-off gate before production go-live.
epics.notes: Security pen test must pass before Launch Gate go/no-go. Auth endpoints, payment webhook, slot-lock API, and admin console all in scope.

epics.name: Vehicle Type Data Model
epics.description: vehicle_types table (make, model, size class: sedan/SUV/truck), FK on bookings table, per-vehicle service pricing rules, per-vehicle duration, car wash portal bay assignment logic driven by vehicle type.
epics.notes: Unblocks EP-13 sprint planning. Schema change propagates to bookings table, payments table, and car wash portal calendar. Phase 2 only.

epics.name: Analytics & GA4 Integration
epics.description: Engineering implementation of GA4 event instrumentation: consumer app funnel (first_open → booking_confirmed), business dashboard onboarding funnel, real-time ops monitoring dashboard wired to CloudWatch + PagerDuty, ops verification queue SLA tracking events.
epics.notes: GA4 strategy doc already produced. This epic is the engineering build. Must be live before launch to measure Day-1 funnel. GA4 hashing: consumer user_id = MD5(phone).

epics.name: Multi-Language Content
epics.description: Dynamic language switching for business profile content (AR/EN) in consumer app based on US-003 preference. WhatsApp notification language follows consumer preference. Business dashboard dual-language content input for all text fields.
epics.notes: Cross-cuts EP-02, EP-06, EP-07, EP-08. Consumer language preference set in US-003. Without this, English-preference users see Arabic-only profiles — broken UX.

epics.name: Launch Readiness & QA
epics.description: Sprint 9 formal stories: load test spec and execution (k6, 100 concurrent flows), App Store + Play Store submission checklist (Arabic RTL metadata, privacy policy URL, age rating), Arabic copy final audit by native speaker, WABA production cutover sign-off, pilot business UAT (20 businesses, no eng support).
epics.notes: Sprint 9 stories. Previously undocumented. Each story maps directly to one of the 7 MVP Launch Gates in the PRD.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to register with my Egyptian phone number so I can create an account without an email.
acceptance_criteria: 1. OTP sent to +20 mobile within 5 seconds.
2. OTP expires in 5 minutes.
3. 5 failed attempts trigger 15-min lockout.
4. Phone number stored in E.164 format.
5. Success redirects to profile setup.
stories.notes: Phone must accept +20 prefix. Also international.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to sign in with Google or Apple so I can log in without entering a phone number.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Apple Sign-In shown on iOS (App Store requirement).
2. Google shown on both iOS and Android.
3. First login creates account; repeat login signs in.
4. Social login fetches display name and email.
5. Arabic UI throughout flow.
stories.notes: Apple Sign-In is mandatory for iOS App Store compliance.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to set up my profile (name, language, notification preferences) so the app is personalised for me.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Name field: Arabic or Latin, required.
2. Language toggle: Arabic (default) / English.
3. Notification preferences: WhatsApp on/off, push on/off.
4. Profile photo optional (S3 upload).
5. Saved and shown in profile tab.
stories.notes: Language preference drives all subsequent UI and notification language.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to log in with my phone number or email so I can access my dashboard securely.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Phone OTP login: same flow as consumer.
2. Email + password login: email verified on first setup.
3. Session persists 30 days on trusted device.
4. Password reset via SMS OTP.
5. Dashboard shown immediately after login.
stories.notes: Separate from consumer login UI but shared backend auth service.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to set up my business profile (name, location, photos, description) so customers can find and trust my business.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Name required in Arabic AND English.
2. Min 3 photos uploaded (S3).
3. Location: address text + Google Maps pin confirmation.
4. Category selection: restaurant/salon/court/gaming/carwash.
5. Profile saved with status PENDING (awaiting ops verification).
stories.notes: Ops must verify before profile goes ACTIVE. SLA: 24 hours.

stories.user_story: As a logged-in consumer, I want to log out from any device so I can secure my account.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Logout clears JWT and refresh token.
2. Session invalidated server-side.
3. Redirect to login screen.
4. Push notification token deregistered on logout.
stories.notes: Standard auth flow.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to verify a newly registered business within 24 hours so only real businesses appear on the platform.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New businesses appear in PENDING queue in admin console.
2. Ops can view: name, category, location, photos, phone.
3. Approve: status → ACTIVE, WhatsApp notification sent to owner.
4. Reject: reason required, WhatsApp notification with reason sent.
5. SLA alert if business pending > 24 hours.
stories.notes: Core trust and safety gate. No business auto-activates.

stories.user_story: As a returning consumer, I want to reset my password via SMS OTP so I can regain access to my account.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Forgot password' on login screen.
2. OTP sent to registered phone.
3. OTP valid 5 minutes.
4. New password set: minimum 8 characters.
5. All existing sessions invalidated on reset.
stories.notes: Standard reset flow.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see a personalised home feed of businesses near me so I can quickly find something to book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Home feed shows businesses within 10km sorted by proximity × rating × availability.
2. Category tiles at top: restaurant, salon, court, gaming, car wash.
3. Featured businesses (max 3) shown above organic results.
4. Feed loads within 1.5 seconds on 4G.
5. Skeleton screen shown during load.
stories.notes: Featured tile logic linked to EP-17 but feed itself is Phase 1.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to search for restaurants by name, district, date, and party size so I can find what I need quickly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Search bar on home and search tab.
2. Auto-complete suggestions after 2 characters.
3. Results filtered by: category, district, date, party size, rating ≥ N.
4. Arabic and transliterated Arabic supported.
5. Empty state with Arabic 'عدّل البحث' CTA shown when no results.
stories.notes: Arabic full-text search using PostgreSQL tsvector or Elasticsearch.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to view a business profile with photos, rating, menu/services, and available slots so I can decide whether to book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Profile shows: photos (min 3, lazy loaded), name AR+EN, description AR+EN, rating (after ≥5 reviews), review count, location map, services list with prices.
2. Next 3 available slots shown without entering booking flow.
3. 'Book Now' sticky CTA at bottom.
4. Map link opens Google Maps.
stories.notes: Star rating shown only after 5 reviews — display 'New' badge before that.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to filter search results by cuisine type, section, and rating so I can narrow down my options.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Filter panel: cuisine type (restaurants), service type (salons), rating (≥3/4/5), price range, indoor/outdoor.
2. Filters persist during session.
3. Active filter count badge shown on filter button.
4. Clear all filters with one tap.
5. Results refresh without full page reload.
stories.notes: Max 6 simultaneous filters.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see a Google Map with nearby businesses pinned so I can choose based on proximity.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Map tab on search results.
2. Business pins on map using category colour.
3. Tapping pin shows mini-profile card.
4. 'Book' button on mini-card opens full profile.
5. Map bounding box re-queries as user pans.
stories.notes: Phase 1 stretch. Can launch without map view.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a ranking algorithm to sort search results by proximity × rating × availability density so consumers see the best options first.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Base score = (1/distance_km) × (rating/5) × (available_slots/total_slots).
2. Featured businesses get +30% score boost.
3. New businesses (< 5 reviews) get +10% boost for first 30 days.
4. Algorithm weights documented and configurable via admin console.
5. P95 query time < 500ms.
stories.notes: Algorithm weights tuned with real data post-launch.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see recently visited businesses on my home feed so I can quickly rebook.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Recently visited' horizontal scroll strip below category tiles.
2. Shows last 5 businesses where consumer completed a booking.
3. Strip hidden until first completed booking.
4. Tapping opens business profile directly.
stories.notes: Requires booking history. Surfaced after first completed booking.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see real-time slot availability for a restaurant so I know which times I can book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Available slots fetched in real-time (not cached).
2. Slot shows: time, duration, available capacity.
3. Full slots shown in strikethrough/greyed.
4. Slots refresh if user stays on screen > 2 minutes.
5. API P95 < 200ms.
stories.notes: Redis caches availability grid; invalidated on each booking.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to book a restaurant with occasion, party size, section, and special requests so the restaurant can prepare for my visit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Step 1: Occasion selector (birthday, anniversary, business, casual).
2. Step 2: Party size stepper 1–20.
3. Step 3: Date + time slot selection.
4. Step 4: Section preference (indoor/outdoor/private/any).
5. Step 5: Special requests free text (max 200 chars).
6. Step 6: Booking summary with deposit + platform fee breakdown.
7. All labels and CTAs in Arabic.
stories.notes: Core Phase 1 journey. Must be perfect before launch.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to book a salon appointment selecting service type, stylist preference, and time so I always get the right person.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Step 1: Service type from business service list (with price + duration).
2. Step 2: Add-on services (combinable; total duration + price auto-calculated).
3. Step 3: Stylist preference — 'Any available' or specific stylist.
4. Step 4: Date + time (filtered by stylist availability if specific stylist selected).
5. If preferred stylist unavailable: show next available slot for that stylist (do NOT silently reassign).
6. Booking summary with breakdown.
stories.notes: Staff-resource model: each stylist is a resource with their own calendar.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to hold a selected slot for 8 minutes during checkout so the consumer can complete payment without losing it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Redis SETNX lock acquired on slot selection (key: SLOT_HOLD:{slot_id}).
2. TTL set to 480 seconds.
3. Countdown timer shown to consumer (8:00 countdown).
4. If timer expires: slot released, consumer shown 'الوقت انتهى' screen with alternative slots.
5. On payment success: Redis lock deleted, slot.booked_count incremented atomically.
stories.notes: Redis distributed lock is non-negotiable. Must be tested with 100 concurrent users.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a booking state machine with defined transitions so booking status is always consistent and auditable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. States: PENDING_PAYMENT → CONFIRMED → COMPLETED | CANCELLED_BY_CONSUMER | CANCELLED_BY_BUSINESS | NO_SHOW | DISPUTED.
2. Invalid transitions return HTTP 409.
3. Every state change is logged with timestamp and reason.
4. State visible on business dashboard and consumer booking history.
5. State drives all notification triggers.
stories.notes: State machine must be unit-tested with all transition paths.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to reschedule my booking to a different slot so I don't have to cancel and rebook.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reschedule available only if: new slot available AND original booking is outside cancellation window.
2. Original deposit transfers to new booking.
3. Reschedule limit: 2 times per booking.
4. Third attempt: shown 'Cancel and rebook' option.
5. Business receives WhatsApp notification of reschedule.
stories.notes: Deposit transfer logic: escrow updated to new booking_id.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to cancel my booking and understand whether I get a refund so I can make an informed decision.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Cancel outside window: full deposit refund shown, confirm CTA shown.
2. Cancel inside window: policy shown ('العربون لن يُسترد'), confirm CTA with destructive style.
3. Cancellation reason optional dropdown (changed mind, emergency, other).
4. Refund initiated same day.
5. WhatsApp confirmation sent with refund amount and ETA.
stories.notes: Cancellation window is set per-business (default 24h).

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want conflict detection to prevent double-booking a slot, stylist, or consumer time so booking integrity is maintained.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Slot capacity exceeded: hard block, HTTP 409, show alternatives.
2. Staff double-booking (salon): hard block, show next available slot for that staff.
3. Consumer booking same time at two businesses: soft warning, allow override.
4. All checks happen within slot hold acquisition (atomic with Redis lock).
5. Conflicts logged for ops review.
stories.notes: Conflicts must be tested in load tests.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to add a manual booking for a walk-in or phone call so all my bookings are in one place.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Dashboard 'Add manual booking' button.
2. Fields: slot selection, guest name, guest phone, party size, deposit waived toggle.
3. Manual booking shown in same calendar view as online bookings.
4. Deposit waived = no payment collected; escrow not created.
5. Guest NOT notified (manual booking is internal record).
stories.notes: Walk-in bookings are common in Egypt. Essential for business adoption.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to receive a booking reference immediately after confirmation so I can identify my booking.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Booking ref format: BK-YYYYMMDD-XXXXX (5-char alphanumeric, unique).
2. Shown on confirmation screen in monospace font.
3. Included in WhatsApp confirmation.
4. Searchable in My Bookings.
5. Business sees same ref in dashboard.
stories.notes: 5-char suffix generated with UUID truncation + collision check.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to view all my upcoming and past bookings in one place so I can manage my schedule.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'My Bookings' tab: Upcoming and Past sub-tabs.
2. Each booking card shows: business name, date/time, status badge, booking ref.
3. Upcoming: reschedule + cancel actions.
4. Past: review CTA if not yet reviewed.
5. Empty state Arabic copy per tab.
stories.notes: Standard list view. Pagination after 20 items.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to configure my availability calendar with operating hours, slot duration, and capacity so only correct slots are shown to consumers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Day-by-day operating hours (open/close time per day).
2. Slot duration: 60/90/120 min (restaurant default: 90).
3. Simultaneous capacity per slot (number of tables/chairs).
4. Blackout dates (e.g., Eid closure).
5. Changes take effect from the NEXT calendar day.
stories.notes: Critical for Phase 1 launch. Business must configure before going live.

stories.user_story: As a salon owner, I want to assign services to specific staff members so consumers can only book the right person for each service.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Staff management screen: add staff name, photo, specialisations.
2. Service list: assign each service to one or more staff.
3. Booking engine only shows staff available for selected service.
4. Staff cannot be double-booked (enforced by conflict detection).
5. Staff can be deactivated (hides from consumer, preserves history).
stories.notes: Salon category only. Core differentiation of salon journey.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to pay my booking deposit using InstaPay, card, Fawry, or Vodafone Cash so I can use my preferred payment method.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Payment screen shows: InstaPay, Meeza card, Visa/Mastercard, Fawry, Vodafone Cash.
2. All methods integrated via Paymob.
3. Currency: EGP only.
4. Payment amount clearly shown: deposit + platform booking fee (itemised).
5. Processing indicator during payment.
6. Success: booking confirmed. Failure: retry or change method.
stories.notes: Paymob sandbox from Day 1. Production account requires 2-week approval.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to hold all consumer deposits in escrow until the booking is completed so both parties are protected.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Deposit held in Paymob escrow on booking confirmation.
2. NOT transferred to business until booking is COMPLETED.
3. On COMPLETED: payout triggered within 48 hours via Paymob disbursement.
4. On cancellation outside window: full refund to consumer within 3–5 business days.
5. All escrow movements logged in payments table.
stories.notes: Paymob handles escrow. Platform manages the trigger logic.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to store my card on file so future no-show protection works without requiring upfront payment.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Save card for no-show protection' option at checkout.
2. Card tokenised via Paymob (PCI-DSS compliant — no raw card data on platform).
3. Token stored in users table.
4. Charged only if no-show detected.
5. User can remove saved card from profile settings.
stories.notes: Critical for no-show protection on businesses that don't require upfront deposit.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to receive an Arabic receipt after payment so I have a record of my transaction.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Receipt sent to consumer WhatsApp within 30 seconds of payment.
2. Receipt shows: booking ref, business name, amount paid (deposit + fee), refund policy.
3. Receipt also downloadable from booking detail screen as PDF.
4. All amounts in EGP with Arabic numeral option.
5. Receipt in Arabic with English option.
stories.notes: PDF generation via server-side rendering.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to set a deposit amount and cancellation window so my no-show protection rules are clear to consumers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Deposit amount: fixed EGP amount OR percentage of service value (business's choice).
2. Cancellation window: hours before booking (default: 24).
3. Policy preview shown exactly as consumers will see it.
4. Policy changes apply to NEW bookings only (existing bookings grandfathered).
5. Platform fee (EGP 15–50 by category) shown separately from deposit.
stories.notes: Policy clarity is the #1 driver of consumer trust in the deposit model.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to process refunds automatically when a consumer cancels outside the window so no manual ops intervention is required.
acceptance_criteria: 1. On cancel (outside window): refund triggered automatically via Paymob refund API.
2. Consumer receives WhatsApp confirmation of refund + ETA.
3. InstaPay/Fawry: credited to platform wallet (consumer can withdraw).
4. Card: refunded to original card (3–5 business days).
5. Refund record in payments table with status and settled_at.
stories.notes: Idempotency: refund webhook must not double-refund on retry.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to verify all Paymob webhooks with HMAC-SHA512 so no fraudulent payment events are processed.
acceptance_criteria: 1. HMAC secret stored in environment variable (never in code).
2. All incoming webhooks verified before processing.
3. Invalid HMAC: reject with 401, alert ops via CloudWatch.
4. Idempotency: check payments table for existing transaction before processing.
5. Webhook retry from Paymob must not create duplicate records.
stories.notes: Security-critical. Must be pen-tested before launch.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to receive daily payouts to my bank account or Paymob wallet so I get my money reliably.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Payout batch runs daily at 11pm Cairo time.
2. Minimum payout threshold: EGP 50.
3. Business can choose: Egyptian bank transfer or Paymob wallet.
4. Payout statement available in dashboard analytics.
5. Failed payouts trigger WhatsApp alert to business owner.
stories.notes: Below threshold: amounts accumulate to next batch.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to collect the booking fee (EGP 15–50) immediately at checkout so platform revenue is never at risk of refund.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Booking fee charged at same time as deposit (single Paymob transaction).
2. Fee is NON-REFUNDABLE even on cancellation outside window.
3. Fee breakdown shown to consumer on summary screen: 'رسوم الحجز: X ج.م'.
4. Fee amount by category: restaurant EGP 25, salon EGP 15, court EGP 35, gaming EGP 20, car wash EGP 20.
5. Fee settled immediately (not held in escrow).
stories.notes: Fee is separate from deposit in payments table. Different escrow_status.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a scheduled job to detect no-shows every 15 minutes so businesses are protected without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Job runs every 15 minutes via AWS EventBridge Scheduler.
2. Queries: bookings WHERE status='confirmed' AND start_time < NOW()-30min AND completed_at IS NULL.
3. For each match: status → NO_SHOW, no_show_detected_at = NOW().
4. Triggers penalty split, consumer notification, business notification.
5. Increments user.no_show_count. If ≥3: sets deposit_mandatory=true.
stories.notes: Detection window: 30 minutes after slot start. Business can still manually mark attended before job fires.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to automatically split the no-show penalty (75% business / 25% platform) so both parties are compensated without manual work.
acceptance_criteria: 1. On NO_SHOW status: deposit split executed automatically.
2. Business payout: 75% queued for 48-hour settlement.
3. Platform retention: 25% settled immediately.
4. If card-on-file (no upfront deposit): card charged for no-show fee then split.
5. Two payment records created: business_payout + platform_retention.
stories.notes: Paymob handles the split settlement.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want a 24-hour dispute window after a no-show penalty so I can challenge an unfair charge.
acceptance_criteria: 1. WhatsApp notification includes dispute link valid for 24 hours.
2. Dispute form: reason dropdown + optional description.
3. Submitting dispute: status → DISPUTED, all payouts paused.
4. Ops SLA: 72 hours to review and resolve.
5. Consumer notified of outcome via WhatsApp.
stories.notes: Dispute pauses payout. Ops resolves in admin console.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to review disputed no-show charges and issue resolutions so consumers and businesses are treated fairly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Disputes queue in admin console.
2. Each dispute shows: booking record, no-show timestamp, payment trail, consumer reason.
3. Resolution options: Uphold charge (business keeps 75%), Reverse charge (full consumer refund), Partial refund (custom split).
4. Resolution triggers payment execution.
5. Both parties notified via WhatsApp of outcome.
stories.notes: Ops resolves within 72-hour SLA.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want to flag repeat no-show consumers as deposit-mandatory so businesses are protected from serial offenders.
acceptance_criteria: 1. user.no_show_count incremented on each NO_SHOW status.
2. At no_show_count ≥ 3: user.deposit_mandatory = true.
3. Deposit-mandatory users cannot use card-on-file option.
4. Flag visible to business owner in booking details.
5. Flag cannot be cleared by consumer (ops only).
stories.notes: Protects businesses without blocking consumers entirely.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to cancel my booking within the policy window and understand my refund clearly so there are no surprises.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Cancellation button in booking detail.
2. IF outside cancellation window: 'You will receive a full refund of X EGP' shown.
3. IF inside cancellation window: 'This cancellation will forfeit your deposit of X EGP' shown in red with policy explanation.
4. Confirmation requires explicit 'Yes, cancel' tap on destructive button.
5. Both scenarios trigger correct payment flow (US-034 or no-show split).
stories.notes: Cancellation inside window uses same 75/25 split as no-show.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to mark a booking as attended or no-show from my dashboard so the escrow release or penalty is triggered correctly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each booking in calendar has 'Mark Attended' and 'Mark No-Show' buttons.
2. Mark Attended: status → COMPLETED, escrow release triggered.
3. Mark No-Show: status → NO_SHOW, penalty split triggered (same as automated detection).
4. Buttons hidden after status is set.
5. Business cannot change status after setting it (ops only).
stories.notes: Manual mark takes priority over the detection job.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to receive a WhatsApp booking confirmation within 30 seconds of payment so I have immediate proof of my booking.
acceptance_criteria: 1. WhatsApp sent within 30 seconds of CONFIRMED status.
2. Template: booking_confirmation_ar (pre-approved WABA template).
3. Contains: business name AR, date/time AR, booking ref, address, cancel link.
4. If WhatsApp undelivered after 60 seconds: SMS fallback via Twilio.
5. Notification record in database with provider_message_id.
stories.notes: WABA template must be submitted and approved before launch (2–4 weeks).

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want a WhatsApp reminder 24 hours before my booking and a push reminder 2 hours before so I never forget.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 24-hour reminder: WhatsApp with business name, time, address, reschedule/cancel link.
2. 2-hour reminder: push notification (FCM) with business name and time.
3. Both suppressed if booking already cancelled.
4. User can opt out of reminders in profile settings.
5. Reminders queued when booking is confirmed (scheduled via EventBridge).
stories.notes: EventBridge schedules both reminders at booking confirmation time.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want a WhatsApp review prompt 2 hours after my visit so I can easily share my experience.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Sent 2 hours after booking slot end time.
2. Only sent if booking status = COMPLETED.
3. Single-tap star rating link (opens in-app review screen).
4. Template: review_prompt_ar.
5. Only one review prompt per booking.
stories.notes: Review prompt is the trigger for the entire review loop.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want an in-app and WhatsApp notification when a no-show penalty is charged so I understand what happened.
acceptance_criteria: 1. WhatsApp sent within 5 minutes of NO_SHOW status.
2. Shows: business name, penalty amount, 24-hour dispute link.
3. Push notification also sent simultaneously.
4. Notification tone is informational, not accusatory.
5. Opt-out NOT available for penalty notifications.
stories.notes: Cannot be opt-out — financial notification.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want push notifications on my dashboard for new bookings, cancellations, and upcoming bookings so I can manage my operations in real time.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New booking: dashboard push + sound (if app is open).
2. Cancellation: dashboard push + optional SMS.
3. Upcoming booking (2h before): dashboard push.
4. No-show detected: push + email.
5. Business can configure notification preferences in settings.
stories.notes: Dashboard push via FCM (PWA push notifications).

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want all notifications to go through an SQS queue with dead-letter handling so no notification is lost even if a provider is down.
acceptance_criteria: 1. All notifications enqueued in AWS SQS FIFO queue.
2. Workers (WhatsApp, SMS, Push) poll queue and process.
3. Failed delivery: retry with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s).
4. After 3 failures: message moves to Dead Letter Queue.
5. DLQ alerts ops via CloudWatch alarm within 5 minutes.
stories.notes: Decoupled notification architecture prevents booking engine from failing on notification errors.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to receive a 'car is ready' notification trigger so I can notify the customer when their car wash is complete.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Dashboard: 'Mark Ready' button on car wash booking.
2. Tapping Mark Ready: triggers WhatsApp to consumer ('سيارتك جاهزة للاستلام').
3. Template: car_ready_ar.
4. Notification sent within 30 seconds.
5. Button replaced with 'Mark Completed' after Mark Ready.
stories.notes: Car wash category only. Phase 2.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want all WhatsApp Business API template messages to be submitted and approved before launch so we can send notifications from Day 1.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Submit to Meta/360dialog: booking_confirmation_ar, reminder_24h_ar, reminder_2h_push, review_prompt_ar, no_show_consumer_ar, no_show_business_ar, cancellation_confirmed_ar, business_cancelled_ar.
2. Approval target: 48 hours per template.
3. Fallback: if template rejected, resubmit within 24 hours.
4. All templates bilingual (Arabic primary).
5. Approval confirmed before production WABA goes live.
stories.notes: Submit templates at Week 1 of development. 2–4 week Meta approval process.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want a calendar view showing all today's and this week's bookings so I can brief my team at the start of each shift.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Day view (default) and week view toggle.
2. Each booking shown as coloured block: guest name, party size, time.
3. Booking status colour-coded: confirmed (blue), completed (green), no-show (red).
4. Tapping block opens booking detail sheet.
5. Calendar RTL: Sunday on right, text right-aligned.
6. Business owner can block time slots from the calendar.
stories.notes: Tablet-first design. Multi-column view for salon (one column per stylist).

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want an analytics dashboard showing bookings, deposit revenue, no-shows prevented, and revenue protected so I can measure my ROI from the platform.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 4 summary metric cards: total bookings, deposit revenue (EGP), no-shows prevented, revenue protected.
2. Bookings trend line chart (last 30 days).
3. Revenue breakdown bar chart by week.
4. No-show rate vs platform average.
5. Date range filter: last 7/30/90 days.
6. Arabic labels, EGP amounts with Arabic numerals.
stories.notes: This is the Day-14 retention anchor — the report that proves platform ROI.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to view full booking details (guest info, occasion, special requests) so I can prepare appropriately.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Booking detail: guest name, phone (masked), party size, occasion, special requests, deposit status, booking ref, slot time.
2. Phone: tap to call.
3. Mark Attended / Mark No-Show buttons (if slot is today and in the past 30 min).
4. Cancel booking (business-initiated): triggers full consumer refund.
5. Note field: business can add internal notes to any booking.
stories.notes: Phone masking: show last 4 digits only until owner taps 'Reveal'.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to update my operating hours and block specific dates so my availability calendar is always accurate.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Settings screen: operating hours per day (open/close toggle + time picker).
2. Bulk copy: apply Mon to all weekdays.
3. Blackout dates: tap dates on calendar to block.
4. Changes take effect from NEXT calendar day (no retroactive changes to confirmed bookings).
5. Change confirmation: 'تم الحفظ' toast.
stories.notes: Real-time calendar update after saving.

stories.user_story: As a salon owner, I want to manage my staff profiles so consumers can select and book specific stylists.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Staff list: add/edit/deactivate staff.
2. Each staff: name, photo, specialisations (linked services).
3. Deactivated staff: hidden from consumer, existing bookings preserved.
4. Staff utilisation shown in analytics.
5. Staff calendar: day view per staff member on dashboard.
stories.notes: Salon category only.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to manage my service menu with prices and durations so consumers see accurate booking options.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Service list: name AR+EN, price (EGP), duration (minutes).
2. Add/edit/deactivate services.
3. Services assignable to specific staff (salon).
4. Price changes apply to NEW bookings only.
5. Deactivated services hidden from consumer booking journey.
stories.notes: Applicable to salon and car wash categories.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to see a daily revenue summary email so I can track performance without opening the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Daily email at 9am Cairo time.
2. Shows: bookings yesterday, deposit revenue, no-shows, EGP collected.
3. 'View full analytics' CTA link.
4. Email in Arabic.
5. Can be disabled in notification settings.
stories.notes: Email via SES or SendGrid. Arabic HTML email template.

stories.user_story: As a restaurant owner, I want to configure seating sections (indoor, outdoor, private room) and their capacities so consumers can choose their preference.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Sections config: add section name, capacity, and whether it is bookable (or walk-in only).
2. Consumer sees section preference on booking journey step 4.
3. Each section is a resource type — booking assigned to section, not just slot.
4. Analytics: occupancy per section.
5. Sections can be added/removed without disrupting existing bookings.
stories.notes: Restaurant category only.

stories.user_story: As a multi-branch business owner (Growth tier), I want to switch between branches from a single login so I can manage all locations efficiently.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Branch switcher in dashboard header.
2. All availability, bookings, and analytics are branch-scoped.
3. Consolidated analytics view: all branches combined.
4. Staff are branch-specific (not shared).
5. Available on Growth and Pro tiers only.
stories.notes: Growth tier feature. Upsell prompt shown on Starter tier.

stories.user_story: As a new business owner, I want a guided onboarding checklist so I know exactly what to set up before going live.
acceptance_criteria: 1. First-login checklist: profile complete, photos added, operating hours set, deposit rules set, first slot published.
2. Progress indicator (e.g., 3/5 complete).
3. Each item links directly to the relevant settings screen.
4. Checklist dismissed after all items complete or after 30 days.
5. Checklist in Arabic.
stories.notes: Reduces Day-1 churn. Simple UX win.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want a fully Arabic RTL interface so the app feels native to Egyptian users and not a translated foreign product.
acceptance_criteria: 1. All screens render RTL by default.
2. Arabic is the default language on first install.
3. Back arrow on LEFT side of nav header (RTL standard).
4. Arabic-Indic numerals (٠١٢٣) used for all UI counts (party size, slot times, ratings).
5. Western numerals used ONLY for booking refs, EGP amounts inline in Arabic text, and phone numbers.
6. Progress fills right-to-left.
7. Animations: screens slide in from left (RTL convention).
stories.notes: Arabic RTL is the #1 non-negotiable design constraint. All screens audited by native Arabic speaker before launch.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want the app to show skeleton screens instead of spinners so loading feels fast even on slow connections.
acceptance_criteria: 1. All list views and profile screens show skeleton on load.
2. Skeleton matches the layout of the loaded content.
3. Skeleton fills are right-aligned for RTL layouts.
4. Skeleton dismissed when first data chunk received (progressive loading).
5. No spinner used for content loading (spinners only for action processing).
stories.notes: Egyptian 4G has intermittent drops. Perceived performance critical.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want the app to show helpful Arabic empty states so I always understand what to do next.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Empty states required for: No bookings (upcoming + past), No search results, No internet connection, Profile incomplete.
2. Each has: illustration (max 160px), Arabic heading, Arabic body copy, primary CTA button.
3. No internet: 'لا يوجد اتصال بالإنترنت' with retry button.
4. No search results: 'لا توجد نتائج' with 'عدّل البحث' CTA.
5. Empty states reviewed by native Arabic speaker.
stories.notes: Empty states prevent user confusion and abandonment.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want the app to work correctly on common Egyptian Android devices so I can use it without issues.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Tested on: Samsung Galaxy A54, Xiaomi Redmi Note 12, OPPO A78.
2. No layout breaks on any tested device.
3. Arabic font renders correctly on Android (Cairo variable font).
4. RTL scrolling correct on all tested devices.
5. Push notifications work on all tested devices.
stories.notes: Egypt's #1 Android market share: Samsung (40%), Xiaomi (22%), OPPO (12%).

stories.user_story: As a consumer on iOS, I want to sign in with Apple so I can use the app without creating a new account.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Apple Sign-In shown on iOS per App Store requirement.
2. Apple ID name and email pre-fill profile.
3. Privacy email relay supported.
4. Silent login on app relaunch.
5. Apple Sign-In button styled per Apple HIG guidelines.
stories.notes: Apple Sign-In mandatory if any social login is offered on iOS.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to share my booking via WhatsApp so I can notify friends or save it for reference.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Share booking' button on confirmation screen and booking detail.
2. Generates pre-formatted Arabic WhatsApp message: business name, date, time, booking ref.
3. Opens WhatsApp share sheet natively.
4. Message includes: 'حجزت في [business] يوم [date] الساعة [time] — رقم الحجز: [ref]'.
5. Works on iOS and Android.
stories.notes: WhatsApp sharing is the primary viral loop in Egypt.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want a real-time platform health dashboard so I can monitor booking volumes, error rates, and payment success rates.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Dashboard shows: bookings/hour (live), payment success rate, no-show rate, MAU, active businesses, error rate (< 0.1% SLA).
2. Metric cards update every 60 seconds.
3. Alert badges for any metric outside SLA thresholds.
4. Historical trends (last 7 days) per metric.
5. Access restricted to ops admin role.
stories.notes: Datadog powers the ops-level view. Admin console shows business-level view.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to view and approve or reject pending business registrations so only verified businesses appear on the platform.
acceptance_criteria: 1. PENDING queue shows new business signups.
2. Each entry shows: name, category, district, photos, phone number.
3. Ops can call business to verify (phone click-to-call).
4. Approve: status → ACTIVE, WhatsApp notification sent.
5. Reject: reason required, WhatsApp notification with reason.
6. SLA alert if business pending > 24 hours.
stories.notes: Critical trust gate. No auto-approval in Phase 1.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to manage dispute resolution with full payment trail so I can make fair decisions quickly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Disputes queue with: booking record, no-show timestamp, payment amounts, consumer reason.
2. Resolution actions: Uphold, Reverse, Partial refund (custom EGP amount).
3. Resolution comments required.
4. Payment execution triggered automatically on resolution.
5. Both parties notified via WhatsApp.
6. All resolutions logged with ops username and timestamp.
stories.notes: 72-hour SLA. Disputed payouts paused until resolution.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to suspend or deactivate business accounts so I can remove bad actors from the platform.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Suspend: business hidden from search, no new bookings possible.
2. Active bookings NOT cancelled on suspension (handled manually).
3. Suspension reason required.
4. Business notified via WhatsApp with reason.
5. Reactivation possible by Super Admin only.
6. Deactivation is permanent (soft delete, data preserved).
stories.notes: Suspension vs deactivation distinction important for legal/ops.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to manually execute refunds for any booking so I can resolve edge cases the automated system can't handle.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Admin can trigger refund on any booking.
2. Refund reason required.
3. Amount: full deposit or custom EGP amount.
4. Refunds above EGP 500 require Super Admin approval.
5. Refund logged in payments table with admin username.
stories.notes: Approval workflow for large refunds prevents fraud.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to manage featured listing slots so I can assign, schedule, and bill paid placements.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Featured listing slots: per category + district.
2. Admin assigns business to featured slot with start/end date.
3. Billing: linked to business SaaS subscription or manual invoice.
4. Featured label visible on consumer profile and search.
5. Max 3 featured businesses per category per district.
stories.notes: Phase 2. Revenue unlock.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to leave a rating and optional review after my visit so I can help others choose.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Review prompt sent via WhatsApp 2 hours after booking slot end.
2. Tapping prompt opens in-app review screen.
3. Screen: 1–5 star rating (required) + optional text (max 500 chars).
4. Submission creates review with status PENDING.
5. Only consumers with a COMPLETED booking for that business can leave a review.
stories.notes: One review per booking. Verified-booker-only.

stories.user_story: As an ops admin, I want reviews to go through a moderation queue so fake or abusive reviews don't appear on profiles.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New reviews enter PENDING status.
2. Automated spam check (profanity filter, duplicate detection).
3. Reviews passing auto-check: status → APPROVED within 10 minutes.
4. Flagged reviews: ops manual review within 4 hours.
5. Rejected reviews: consumer notified with reason (generic: 'لم يستوفِ معايير النشر').
stories.notes: Review integrity is critical for marketplace trust. Zero tolerance for fake reviews.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see authentic ratings and reviews on business profiles so I can make informed decisions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Rating shown on profile only after ≥5 verified reviews.
2. Before 5 reviews: 'جديد' badge shown instead of rating.
3. Review cards: star rating, text (if provided), date.
4. Consumer name shown as first name + first letter of last name.
5. Latest reviews first (max 10 shown, load more button).
stories.notes: Business cannot respond to reviews in Phase 1.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to see my average rating and recent reviews in my dashboard so I can monitor customer satisfaction.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Analytics shows: average rating trend over time.
2. Recent reviews list (last 10) with star and text.
3. Review count shown.
4. Low-rating alert if rating drops below 3.5 (push notification to owner).
5. Export reviews as CSV from analytics.
stories.notes: Phase 1 stretch. Low priority.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a loyalty points schema in the database so Phase 2 loyalty features can be built on a solid foundation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. loyalty_points table: user_id, booking_id, points, transaction_type (earn/redeem), created_at.
2. user.loyalty_balance column added.
3. Points calculation rule defined: 1 EGP deposit = 1 point (configurable).
4. No consumer-facing loyalty UI in Phase 1 (schema only).
5. Backend ready to calculate and store points from Day 1.
stories.notes: Capturing data from Day 1 means loyalty can launch in Phase 2 with real data.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want a 'Book Again' shortcut on my completed booking so I can rebook a place I liked in one tap.
acceptance_criteria: 1. After review prompt (or 2h post-visit), 'Book Again' CTA shown in app.
2. Tapping pre-fills booking journey with same business.
3. Previous booking preferences pre-populated: party size, occasion.
4. Consumer still selects new date/time.
5. Also visible on past bookings list.
stories.notes: Highest-ROI retention mechanic. Requires completed booking history.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to accept the platform's privacy policy at signup so my consent is recorded and legally compliant.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Privacy policy screen shown after OTP verification, before profile setup.
2. Arabic full text displayed inline (not link only).
3. Consumer must scroll to bottom before 'Accept' CTA activates.
4. Acceptance stored in DB: user_id, policy_version, accepted_at, ip_address.
5. Policy version increments on any material change; existing users re-prompted on next login.
6. Privacy policy URL live before App Store submission (Apple requirement).
stories.notes: Egypt PDPL 2020 + Apple App Store mandatory requirement. Policy URL must be submitted with app review.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to request deletion of my account and personal data so I can exercise my right to erasure under PDPL 2020.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Delete my account' option in Profile > Settings.
2. Consumer shown what will be deleted: name, phone, profile photo, saved card tokens.
3. Consumer shown what will be retained: anonymised booking history (booking_id, date, category, amount — no PII).
4. Confirmation requires typing 'حذف' to proceed.
5. Soft-delete executed: PII fields nulled/hashed, FCM token deregistered, Paymob token invalidated via API.
6. Completion confirmation sent to consumer's phone via SMS.
7. Account deletion irreversible. Ops-only reactivation not possible.
stories.notes: PDPL 2020 Article 17 right to erasure. Booking history retained in anonymised form for financial audit trail.

stories.user_story: As a consumer, I want to export all my personal data so I can see exactly what the platform holds about me.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Download my data' option in Profile > Settings.
2. Request queued; export ready within 24 hours.
3. Consumer notified via WhatsApp when export is ready.
4. Export includes: profile data, booking history (full), payment amounts, reviews submitted.
5. Format: JSON file + human-readable PDF summary.
6. Download link expires after 48 hours.
7. Max 1 export request per 30 days.
stories.notes: PDPL 2020 Article 16 right to access. Generated server-side; S3 presigned URL for download.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want PII fields in the bookings table to be purged after 24 months so data retention obligations are met.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Scheduled job runs monthly (1st of each month, 2am Cairo time).
2. Targets: bookings where completed_at or cancelled_at < NOW() - 24 months.
3. Fields nulled: consumer special_requests, guest_phone (in manual bookings).
4. booking_id, amounts, dates, status, category retained for financial audit.
5. Purge job logs: records_processed, fields_nulled, run_timestamp — stored in audit_logs table.
6. Job is idempotent: safe to re-run.
stories.notes: PDPL 2020 data minimisation principle. 24-month window aligns with Egyptian financial record-keeping requirements.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want OTP requests throttled per phone number so brute-force and SMS-bombing attacks are prevented.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Max 3 OTP requests per phone number per 10-minute window.
2. On 4th request within window: HTTP 429 returned, error message shown: 'لقد تجاوزت الحد المسموح، يرجى الانتظار'.
3. Window tracked in Redis (key: OTP_THROTTLE:{phone}, TTL: 600s).
4. After window expires: counter resets, normal flow resumes.
5. 5 failed OTP verifications (wrong code): 15-minute lockout (existing US-001 AC-3 — confirm aligned).
6. Throttle events logged to CloudWatch with phone hash (not plaintext).
stories.notes: Prevents SMS cost abuse and credential stuffing. Redis-native — no additional infra needed.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want all admin console actions to be written to an immutable audit log so every privileged action is traceable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit log table: action_id (UUID), actor_email, actor_role, action_type, target_entity, target_id, reason (required for destructive actions), ip_address, user_agent, created_at.

2. Action types covered: business_approved, business_rejected, business_suspended, business_reactivated, refund_executed, dispute_resolved, featured_slot_assigned, admin_user_created, admin_role_changed.
3. Audit log rows are INSERT-only — no UPDATE or DELETE permitted (enforced at DB level via row-level security or trigger).
4. Super Admin can VIEW log; cannot modify.
5. Log exported monthly to S3 (cold storage, encrypted).
stories.notes: Immutability enforced at PostgreSQL level. Audit log is the evidence trail for any dispute about ops actions.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want Paymob webhook processing to enforce strict idempotency so duplicate webhook deliveries never create duplicate payment records.
acceptance_criteria: 1. On webhook receipt: extract transaction_id from payload.
2. Query payments table: SELECT id FROM payments WHERE provider_transaction_id = :transaction_id.
3. If record exists: return HTTP 200 immediately with no further processing (idempotent ack).
4. If record does not exist: process normally, insert record with provider_transaction_id populated.
5. provider_transaction_id column has UNIQUE constraint — DB-level hard stop against duplicates.
6. Idempotency check happens AFTER HMAC validation (US-035), BEFORE any business logic.
7. Duplicate detection events logged to CloudWatch.
stories.notes: Complements existing HMAC validation in US-035. Paymob retries webhooks on timeout — this prevents double-charging.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a formal security pen test completed and signed off before production go-live so known vulnerabilities are closed before real consumer data is at risk.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Pen test scope defined: auth endpoints (/auth/otp/*, /auth/social), slot locking service, payment webhook handler (/webhooks/paymob), admin console (all endpoints), consumer booking API.
2. Pen test vendor engaged minimum 2 weeks before target launch date.
3. Critical and High findings: must be remediated and re-tested before launch gate.
4. Medium findings: remediation plan required; can be post-launch within 30 days.
5. Pen test report attached to launch gate sign-off document.
6. Result: PASS = no outstanding Critical/High findings.
stories.notes: Pen test is Launch Gate prerequisite. Vendor selection and scoping must happen in Sprint 6 at latest to allow 2-week engagement.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want a vehicle_types table and FK on bookings so car wash pricing and duration are driven by vehicle size.
acceptance_criteria: 1. vehicle_types table: id (UUID), name_ar, name_en, size_class (ENUM: sedan | suv | pickup | van), created_at.
2. Seed data: Sedan (سيدان), SUV (دفع رباعي), Pickup (بيك أب), Van (ميكروباص).
3. car_wash_services table extended: price_by_vehicle JSONB ({sedan: EGP, suv: EGP, pickup: EGP, van: EGP}), duration_by_vehicle JSONB (same structure).
4. bookings table: vehicle_type_id UUID NULLABLE FK → vehicle_types.id (NULL for non-car-wash categories).
5. Consumer car wash booking journey: step added — select vehicle type before service selection.
6. Bay assignment logic: SUV/Pickup/Van require wide bay (bay.supports_large_vehicles = true).
stories.notes: Phase 2 only. Schema change is additive (nullable FK) — zero impact on Phase 1 booking engine. Unblocks EP-13 sprint planning.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want consumer app GA4 events instrumented from Day 1 so the booking funnel is measurable immediately at launch.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Events fired: first_open (app install), consumer_signup_initiated, consumer_signup_completed (with method: otp|google|apple), booking_initiated (with category, district), slot_selected, checkout_started, payment_attempted (with method), booking_confirmed, booking_cancelled.
2. Each event includes: user_id (MD5 of phone — never raw PII), category, district, booking_ref (on confirmed only).
3. GA4 Measurement Protocol used for server-side events (booking_confirmed, booking_cancelled) to guarantee delivery regardless of app state.
4. Client-side events (first_open, signup_*) use GA4 React Native SDK.
5. All events verified in GA4 DebugView before Sprint 9.
stories.notes: user_id = MD5(phone) per GA4 strategy doc. Never send raw phone to GA4. Server-side events are the reliable baseline.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want business dashboard GA4 events instrumented so the onboarding funnel and churn risk signals are tracked from Day 1.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Events fired: business_signup_initiated, business_onboarding_step_1_completed (profile), business_onboarding_step_2_completed (availability), business_onboarding_step_3_completed (deposit/policy), business_onboarding_completed, business_first_booking_received, business_login (with days_since_last_login), business_availability_updated.
2. user_id = business registration email (deterministic, non-PII in GA4 context).
3. Target funnel: ≥80% of businesses reach business_first_booking_received within 48h of business_onboarding_completed.
4. All events verified in GA4 DebugView before Sprint 9.
stories.notes: These events power the churn prediction model defined in the GA4 strategy doc. Onboarding funnel drop-off is the #1 early warning signal.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want ops verification queue SLA events tracked so the team has visibility into their own 24-hour SLA compliance.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Events fired: ops_business_verification_queued (with category, district), ops_business_verification_started (with time_in_queue_minutes), ops_business_verification_approved (with total_time_minutes), ops_business_verification_rejected (with rejection_reason_category).
2. Events sent server-side via Measurement Protocol when ops takes action in admin console.
3. GA4 custom report: verification queue SLA dashboard — avg time to approval, rejection rate by category, backlog size by hour.
4. Alert configured: if avg time_in_queue > 720 minutes (12h), Slack alert to ops lead.
stories.notes: Ops team cannot manage their SLA without this data. Directly supports Launch Gate 2 (business onboarding within 48h).

stories.user_story: As a consumer who set English as my language preference, I want business profile content to render in English so I can understand what I'm booking.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Business profile screen respects consumer language preference from US-003.
2. If language = English AND business has name_en and description_en populated: render English fields.
3. If English fields are empty: render Arabic fields with a subtle 'Content in Arabic' label (do NOT show empty fields).
4. Services list: name_en shown if available; price always in EGP regardless of language.
5. Language toggle visible on business profile header — consumer can switch inline without going to settings.
stories.notes: English-preference consumers are likely expats or bilingual Cairenes. Broken experience if they see Arabic-only profiles.

stories.user_story: As the platform, I want WhatsApp notification templates to be sent in the consumer's preferred language so every message is readable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. On any notification trigger: fetch consumer.language_preference before selecting template.
2. If language = Arabic: use _ar template variant (e.g. booking_confirmation_ar).
3. If language = English: use _en template variant (e.g. booking_confirmation_en).
4. All 8 WABA templates from US-052 require both _ar and _en variants submitted to Meta.
5. Fallback: if _en template not yet approved, send _ar template with note 'Arabic confirmation sent'.
6. language_preference stored on consumer record — sourced from US-003 profile setup.
stories.notes: Doubles the WABA template submission count (8 → 16). Submit all 16 at Week 1. Meta approval is the critical path.

stories.user_story: As a business owner, I want to enter my business name, description, and service names in both Arabic and English so consumers of both languages see complete content.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Business profile setup (US-005) and service menu (US-058) forms updated: each text field has AR and EN sub-fields.
2. Arabic fields: required. English fields: optional (with placeholder 'اختياري — يُعرض للمستخدمين الإنجليزية').
3. Character limits: name 100 chars per language, description 500 chars per language, service name 80 chars per language.
4. Dashboard preview toggle: 'عربي / English' — shows how content renders in each language.
5. Existing businesses without English content: prompted via dashboard notification to add EN fields (not mandatory for launch).
stories.notes: English content is optional for businesses but required for the consumer-facing language switch to work. Prompt, don't force.

stories.user_story: As the engineering team, I want a formal load test executed before launch so the platform is verified to handle 100 simultaneous bookings without exceeding the 0.1% error rate SLA.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Load test tool: k6 (scripts committed to repo under /tests/load/).
2. Test scenario: 100 virtual users, each completing full booking flow (search → slot select → Redis lock → payment mock → confirmation) simultaneously.
3. Pass criteria: error rate < 0.1%, P95 booking confirmation latency < 2s, Redis lock contention rate < 5%, zero deadlocks.
4. Test runs against staging environment with production-equivalent data volume (10,000 seed businesses, 50,000 seed bookings).
5. Results documented in /docs/load-test-report.md with pass/fail verdict.
6. Must pass before Sprint 9 sign-off.
stories.notes: Directly maps to MVP Launch Gate 6. k6 scripts to be written in Sprint 8, executed in Sprint 9. Staging must mirror production config.

stories.user_story: As the product team, I want a formal Arabic copy audit completed by a native speaker before launch so no RTL errors or incorrect copy reaches consumers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Audit scope: all consumer-facing screens in the React Native app (minimum 30 screens).
2. Auditor profile: native Egyptian Arabic speaker, not a team member.
3. Checklist per screen: RTL layout correct, Arabic-Indic numerals used where required, no LTR text fragments, copy is natural Egyptian Arabic (not formal MSA), CTAs are clear and action-oriented.
4. Findings logged in a shared Google Sheet with screen name, issue description, severity (Critical/Major/Minor).
5. All Critical and Major findings fixed before launch gate.
6. Auditor sign-off document attached to Sprint 9 completion report.
stories.notes: Directly maps to MVP Launch Gate 4. Auditor must be engaged in Sprint 8 so findings can be fixed in Sprint 9. Budget: EGP 2,000–4,000 for freelance auditor.

stories.user_story: As the product team, I want the App Store and Play Store submission checklist completed so the app is approved and live on launch day.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Apple App Store checklist: App Privacy section completed (data types: name, phone, location, payment info), Privacy Policy URL live and reachable, Arabic RTL declared in app metadata, Apple Sign-In implemented (US-067), age rating: 4+, app screenshots in Arabic on iPhone 6.5" and 12.9" iPad.
2. Google Play checklist: Privacy Policy URL live, data safety form completed, target API level ≥ 34, Arabic locale declared.
3. Both submissions made minimum 2 weeks before target launch date.
4. Rejection response plan: 48-hour turnaround on any rejection feedback.
5. TestFlight (iOS) and Internal Testing (Android) builds live and tested with pilot businesses in Sprint 8.
stories.notes: Directly maps to MVP Launch Gate 4. Apple review is 1–3 days; allow buffer. Privacy Policy from US-081 must be live before submission.

stories.user_story: As the ops team, I want WABA production cutover completed and all 16 templates live before launch so WhatsApp notifications work from the first booking.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 360dialog production WABA account fully registered (not sandbox).
2. All 16 message templates (8 AR + 8 EN from US-052 + US-094) approved by Meta.
3. Production WABA credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager (not .env files).
4. End-to-end test: trigger each template type from staging against a real phone number and verify delivery.
5. SMS fallback (Twilio) tested: disable WABA in staging config, verify booking confirmation falls back to SMS within 60 seconds.
6. Sign-off: ops lead confirms all 16 templates deliverable before Sprint 9 ends.
stories.notes: Directly maps to MVP Launch Gate (WABA live). Template approval is 2–4 weeks — submit all 16 in Sprint 0/1. Any rejection must be resubmitted within 24h.

stories.user_story: As the ops team, I want a pilot business UAT session completed with 20 real businesses before soft launch so the dashboard is validated by real owners without engineering support.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 20 pilot businesses selected across 4 districts (5 per district) and 2 categories (restaurants + salons, min 8 of each).
2. UAT script: business owner completes onboarding checklist (US-062), configures availability (US-027), sets deposit policy (US-033), receives first test booking, marks it attended (US-044).
3. Engineering team NOT present during UAT sessions (simulates real usage).
4. Issues found: logged in Jira with severity. Critical issues block launch. Major issues require fix within 48h.
5. Success criterion: ≥80% of 20 businesses complete full UAT script without support intervention.
6. Ops team debriefs within 24h of each UAT session.
stories.notes: Directly maps to MVP Launch Gate 2. UAT sessions must be scheduled in Sprint 8, completed by end of Sprint 9. Ops lead owns scheduling.

EP-01.user_story: As a consumer, I want to register with my Egyptian phone number so I can create an account without an email.
acceptance_criteria: 1. OTP sent to +20 mobile within 5 seconds.
2. OTP expires in 5 minutes.
3. 5 failed attempts trigger 15-min lockout.
4. Phone number stored in E.164 format.
5. Success redirects to profile setup.
EP-01.notes: Phone must accept +20 prefix. Also international.

EP-01.user_story: As a consumer, I want to sign in with Google or Apple so I can log in without entering a phone number.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Apple Sign-In shown on iOS (App Store requirement).
2. Google shown on both iOS and Android.
3. First login creates account; repeat login signs in.
4. Social login fetches display name and email.
5. Arabic UI throughout flow.
EP-01.notes: Apple Sign-In is mandatory for iOS App Store compliance.

EP-01.user_story: As a consumer, I want to set up my profile (name, language, notification preferences) so the app is personalised for me.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Name field: Arabic or Latin, required.
2. Language toggle: Arabic (default) / English.
3. Notification preferences: WhatsApp on/off, push on/off.
4. Profile photo optional (S3 upload).
5. Saved and shown in profile tab.
EP-01.notes: Language preference drives all subsequent UI and notification language.

EP-01.user_story: As a business owner, I want to log in with my phone number or email so I can access my dashboard securely.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Phone OTP login: same flow as consumer.
2. Email + password login: email verified on first setup.
3. Session persists 30 days on trusted device.
4. Password reset via SMS OTP.
5. Dashboard shown immediately after login.
EP-01.notes: Separate from consumer login UI but shared backend auth service.

EP-01.user_story: As a business owner, I want to set up my business profile (name, location, photos, description) so customers can find and trust my business.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Name required in Arabic AND English.
2. Min 3 photos uploaded (S3).
3. Location: address text + Google Maps pin confirmation.
4. Category selection: restaurant/salon/court/gaming/carwash.
5. Profile saved with status PENDING (awaiting ops verification).
EP-01.notes: Ops must verify before profile goes ACTIVE. SLA: 24 hours.

EP-01.user_story: As a logged-in consumer, I want to log out from any device so I can secure my account.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Logout clears JWT and refresh token.
2. Session invalidated server-side.
3. Redirect to login screen.
4. Push notification token deregistered on logout.

EP-01.user_story: As an ops admin, I want to verify a newly registered business within 24 hours so only real businesses appear on the platform.
acceptance_criteria: 1. New businesses appear in PENDING queue in admin console.
2. Ops can view: name, category, location, photos, phone.
3. Approve: status → ACTIVE, WhatsApp notification sent to owner.
4. Reject: reason required, WhatsApp notification with reason sent.
5. SLA alert if business pending > 24 hours.
EP-01.notes: Core trust and safety gate. No business auto-activates.

EP-01.user_story: As a returning consumer, I want to reset my password via SMS OTP so I can regain access to my account.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Forgot password' on login screen.
2. OTP sent to registered phone.
3. OTP valid 5 minutes.
4. New password set: minimum 8 characters.
5. All existing sessions invalidated on reset.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see a personalised home feed of businesses near me so I can quickly find something to book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Home feed shows businesses within 10km sorted by proximity × rating × availability.
2. Category tiles at top: restaurant, salon, court, gaming, car wash.
3. Featured businesses (max 3) shown above organic results.
4. Feed loads within 1.5 seconds on 4G.
5. Skeleton screen shown during load.
EP-02.notes: Featured tile logic linked to EP-17 but feed itself is Phase 1.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to search for restaurants by name, district, date, and party size so I can find what I need quickly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Search bar on home and search tab.
2. Auto-complete suggestions after 2 characters.
3. Results filtered by: category, district, date, party size, rating ≥ N.
4. Arabic and transliterated Arabic supported.
5. Empty state with Arabic 'عدّل البحث' CTA shown when no results.
EP-02.notes: Arabic full-text search using PostgreSQL tsvector or Elasticsearch.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to view a business profile with photos, rating, menu/services, and available slots so I can decide whether to book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Profile shows: photos (min 3, lazy loaded), name AR+EN, description AR+EN, rating (after ≥5 reviews), review count, location map, services list with prices.
2. Next 3 available slots shown without entering booking flow.
3. 'Book Now' sticky CTA at bottom.
4. Map link opens Google Maps.
EP-02.notes: Star rating shown only after 5 reviews — display 'New' badge before that.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to filter search results by cuisine type, section, and rating so I can narrow down my options.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Filter panel: cuisine type (restaurants), service type (salons), rating (≥3/4/5), price range, indoor/outdoor.
2. Filters persist during session.
3. Active filter count badge shown on filter button.
4. Clear all filters with one tap.
5. Results refresh without full page reload.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see a Google Map with nearby businesses pinned so I can choose based on proximity.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Map tab on search results.
2. Business pins on map using category colour.
3. Tapping pin shows mini-profile card.
4. 'Book' button on mini-card opens full profile.
5. Map bounding box re-queries as user pans.
EP-02.notes: Phase 1 stretch. Can launch without map view.

EP-02.user_story: As the platform, I want a ranking algorithm to sort search results by proximity × rating × availability density so consumers see the best options first.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Base score = (1/distance_km) × (rating/5) × (available_slots/total_slots).
2. Featured businesses get +30% score boost.
3. New businesses (< 5 reviews) get +10% boost for first 30 days.
4. Algorithm weights documented and configurable via admin console.
5. P95 query time < 500ms.
EP-02.notes: Algorithm weights tuned with real data post-launch.

EP-02.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see recently visited businesses on my home feed so I can quickly rebook.
acceptance_criteria: 1. 'Recently visited' horizontal scroll strip below category tiles.
2. Shows last 5 businesses where consumer completed a booking.
3. Strip hidden until first completed booking.
4. Tapping opens business profile directly.
EP-02.notes: Requires booking history. Surfaced after first completed booking.

EP-03.user_story: As a consumer, I want to see real-time slot availability for a restaurant so I know which times I can book.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Available slots fetched in real-time (not cached).
2. Slot shows: time, duration, available capacity.
3. Full slots shown in strikethrough/greyed.
4. Slots refresh if user stays on screen > 2 minutes.
5. API P95 < 200ms.
EP-03.notes: Redis caches availability grid; invalidated on each booking.

EP-03.user_story: As a consumer, I want to book a restaurant with occasion, party size, section, and special requests so the restaurant can prepare for my visit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Step 1: Occasion selector (birthday, anniversary, business, casual).
2. Step 2: Party size stepper 1–20.
3. Step 3: Date + time slot selection.
4. Step 4: Section preference (indoor/outdoor/private/any).
5. Step 5: Special requests free text (max 200 chars).
6. Step 6: Booking summary with deposit + platform fee breakdown.
7. All labels and CTAs in Arabic.
EP-03.notes: Core Phase 1 journey. Must be perfect before launch.

EP-03.user_story: As a consumer, I want to book a salon appointment selecting service type, stylist preference, and time so I always get the right person.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Step 1: Service type from business service list (with price + duration).
2. Step 2: Add-on services (combinable; total duration + price auto-calculated).
3. Step 3: Stylist preference — 'Any available' or specific stylist.
4. Step 4: Date + time (filtered by stylist availability if specific stylist selected).
5. If preferred stylist unavailable: show next available slot for that stylist (do NOT silently reassign).
6. Booking summary with breakdown.
EP-03.notes: Staff-resource model: each stylist is a resource with their own calendar.

EP-03.user_story: As the platform, I want to hold a selected slot for 8 minutes during checkout so the consumer can complete payment without losing it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Redis SETNX lock acquired on slot selection (key: SLOT_HOLD:{slot_id}).
2. TTL set to 480 seconds.
3. Countdown timer shown to consumer (8:00 countdown).
4. If timer expires: slot released, consumer shown 'الوقت انتهى' screen with alternative slots.
5. On payment success: Redis lock deleted, slot.booked_count incremented atomically.
EP-03.notes: Redis distributed lock is non-negotiable. Must be tested with 100 concurrent users.

EP-03.user_story: As the platform, I want a booking state machine with defined transitions so booking status is always consistent and auditable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. States: PENDING_PAYMENT → CONFIRMED → COMPLETED | CANCELLED_BY_CONSUMER | CANCELLED_BY_BUSINESS | NO_SHOW | DISPUTED.
2. Invalid transitions return HTTP 409.
3. Every state change is logged with timestamp and reason.
4. State visible on business dashboard and consumer booking history.
5. State drives all notification triggers.
EP-03.notes: State machine must be unit-tested with all transition paths.

EP-03.user_story: As a consumer, I want to reschedule my booking to a different slot so I don't have to cancel and rebook.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reschedule available only if: new slot available AND original booking is outside cancellation window.
2. Original deposit transfers to new booking.
3. Reschedule limit: 2 times per booking.
4. Third attempt: shown 'Cancel and rebook' option.
5. Business receives WhatsApp notification of reschedule.
EP-03.notes: Deposit transfer logic: escrow updated to new booking_id.

EP-03.user_story: As a consumer, I want to cancel my booking and understand whether I get a refund so I can make an informed decision.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Cancel outside window: full deposit refund shown, confirm CTA shown.
2. Cancel inside window: policy shown ('العربون لن يُسترد'), confirm CTA with destructive style.
3. Cancellation reason optional dropdown (changed mind, emergency, other).
4. Refund initiated same day.
5. WhatsApp confirmation sent with refund amount and ETA.

EP-03.user_story: As the platform, I want conflict detection to prevent double-booking a slot, stylist, or consumer time so booking integrity is maintained.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Slot capacity exceeded: hard block, HTTP 409, show alternatives.
2. Staff double-booking (salon): hard block, show next available slot for that staff.
3. Consumer booking same time at two businesses: soft warning, allow override.
4. All checks happen within slot hold acquisition (atomic with Redis lock).
5. Conflicts logged for ops review.
EP-03.notes: Conflicts must be tested in load tests.
